promotionRule.secondRepeat: Add a promise candidate or explain why it cannot be deterministic yet.
promotionRule.thirdRepeat: Promote the promise or record the missing primitive, access, or observability that blocks promotion.

promises.claim: The self-improving harness promise registry is structurally valid.
promises.whenToUse: After editing docs/context/HARNESS_PROMISES.json or the harness contract.
passRequires: registry JSON parses
promise ids are unique and stable
required fields are present
evidence classes and cost values are canonical
report JSON has ok true
nextSuggestedSteps: Fix the first reported registry error and rerun the validator.

promises.claim: Durable context docs agree with docs/context/ACTIVE_STATE.json and current proof-order rules.
promises.whenToUse: After context edits or before trusting durable next actions from docs.
passRequires: context sync command exits 0
reports/context/latest/context-sync-result.json has ok true
no stale current next actions are reported
nextSuggestedSteps: Run with --fix only for generated ACTIVE_STATE drift.
Demote or rewrite stale claims instead of trusting narrative text.

promises.claim: The active agent loop has no cheap local loop-copilot blocker before slow runtime work, rebuild-heavy work, or final claims.
promises.whenToUse: Before meaningful native, bridge, wake-word, hot-op, runtime-control, release, rebuild-heavy work, or final claims.
passRequires: loop copilot command exits 0
reports/context/latest/loop-copilot-signals.json has ok true
no blocker signal is present
the worker treats any warning as checkpoint guidance, not runtime proof
nextSuggestedSteps: If a blocker is emitted, run the named proof or demote the claim before proceeding.
If only warnings are emitted, include the warning in Builder/Watcher/Gatekeeper review before final claims.
If runtime-control work is visible, prefer compact live introspection/control before rebuilding.

promises.claim: The Windows release feed matches the verified final NSIS installer metadata.
promises.whenToUse: Before asking Go Native or Check Update to see a Windows build.
passRequires: native/windows/release/VERIFY.json exists
feed buildId matches verified installer buildId
feed SHA-256 matches verified installer SHA-256
local published installer bytes match feed metadata
nextSuggestedSteps: If VERIFY.json is missing, rebuild and verify the final NSIS installer before claiming feed proof.
If the feed mismatches, regenerate the release feed from verified package metadata.

promises.claim: The installed Windows native-control bridge exposes the required hot-op shell capabilities.
promises.whenToUse: Before doctor, Android wake proof, or any ADB-backed Android work through the Windows bridge.
passRequires: installed Windows app is running
local bridge responds
shellProtocolVersion and hotOpsProtocolVersion match docs
required shell capabilities are present
canary hot operation returns success
nextSuggestedSteps: If bridge_update_required or hot_operation_missing appears, fix bridge/hot-op loading before blaming Android runtime.
If the installed app is absent, install the feed-published shell first.

promises.claim: The Android runtime behavior named by the simulator report passed.
promises.whenToUse: When a claim depends on Android runtime behavior rather than APK package metadata.
passRequires: simulator command exits 0
report names the exact behavior proven
report status is PASS
claim does not exceed the behavior named by the report
nextSuggestedSteps: If the simulator is unavailable, classify as invalid-environment instead of Android-disconnected.
Run a behavior-specific local report when reusing external device evidence.

promises.claim: Android Gradle rebuilds run the UX performance regression guard before build work proceeds.
promises.whenToUse: Before any Android rebuild, after changing Android startup/touch/shell code, or when checking that incremental native work cannot skip the UX regression guard.
passRequires: native/android/app/build.gradle declares verifyAndroidUxPerformanceRegression
preBuild depends on verifyAndroidUxPerformanceRegression
the guard command is plugins/wasm-agent/tests/android_lite_performance_budget.test.js
the guard includes Android shell v2 size, bridge, loadUrl, and forbidden-startup budgets
the guard requires NativeShellV2Activity to own the installed launcher/auth-return routes while legacy MainActivity is not the normal relaunch path
the guard requires Android input-budget proof to budget Activity launch timings
the guard requires the deterministic release loop to reuse the existing feed on --skip-build unless --publish-feed is explicit
the guard requires the deterministic release loop to accept install proof only when APK SHA/build diagnostics match the promoted release
the guard requires shell-v2 ADB relaunch proof to be explicit opt-in and launch-only without force-stop or synthetic swipe
the guard requires the UX release-loop install phase to use the Android UI input hot-op install action rather than the legacy voice-tuning reinstall command
Gradle :app:preBuild executes :app:verifyAndroidUxPerformanceRegression
the guard prints android lite performance budget ok
nextSuggestedSteps: If the promise fails, fix the guard or the Gradle preBuild hook before rebuilding.
If an Android feature needs to exceed a budget, split it into a later v2 layer with a new explicit gate instead of raising layer-0 budgets silently.
This promise is not runtime UX proof; after an installed APK change, still run android-native-input-budget or android-native-ux-release-loop.

promises.claim: Android native canvas/navigation input stays inside the configured frame/input budget after a real ADB swipe through the Windows bridge.
promises.whenToUse: When Android native feels laggy, after changing PWA/native interaction code, or before resuming wake-word tuning that depends on responsive UI.
passRequires: Windows native-control bridge is reachable
run_android_ui_input_proof hot operation is available
gfxinfo reset succeeds
Activity launch total/wait time is present and within the configured budget when launched through am start -W
real ADB swipe succeeds unless --launch-only is explicitly selected
post-swipe gfxinfo has high input latency, slow UI thread, missed frame deadline, slow draw-command, jank, and p95 values at or below configured budget unless --launch-only is explicitly selected
nextSuggestedSteps: Use --launch-component com.colmeio.wasmagent/.shell.NativeShellV2Activity only to isolate shell-v2 proof; normal app icon/relaunch proof must still resolve to shell v2.
If high input latency is nonzero, inspect event/listener/main-thread pressure before wake tuning.
If slow draw commands or missed deadlines dominate, simplify canvas paint/layout/scroll invalidation before adding more handlers.
If the bridge is unavailable, run windows-hot-shell-proof first instead of treating Android as disconnected.

promises.claim: The Android native UX release candidate was built or reused, published, installed through the Windows bridge, and proven against the real-device input budget as one deterministic loop.
promises.whenToUse: After Android native responsiveness source changes, before repeating any build/install/input-proof sequence, or whenever a source fix needs installed Android runtime proof.
passRequires: exactly one aggregate release-loop report is emitted under reports/android/responsiveness
local wasm-agent backend starts and /native/control/clients becomes reachable, or startup is explicitly skipped only when already reachable
the promoted APK/feed has a build id, SHA-256, and size
Windows hot-shell proof passes or is explicitly skipped only when already proven in the same active loop
--skip-build reuses the existing release feed unless --publish-feed is explicitly requested
shell-v2 proof blocks when the release feed still publishes an older Android UI input-proof hot-op without component launch support
shell-v2 mode does not perform a second ADB relaunch/input proof unless --run-shell-v2-adb-proof is explicit
the release-loop install phase uses run_android_ui_input_proof install_apk and does not perform a post-install monkey launch
explicit shell-v2 ADB proof is launch-only and avoids force-stop, swipe, and gfxinfo probes

the APK install through the Windows bridge passes, or the install phase/reused report shows matching APK SHA and Android build diagnostics for the promoted build, unless the run is explicitly --skip-install
real-device Android input-budget proof passes unless the run is explicitly --skip-input-budget
manual repeated rebuild/reinstall/proof commands are not used as the primary evidence for the same loop
nextSuggestedSteps: Use --skip-build only when the promoted APK/feed already contains the source change under test.
Do not refresh the feed during --skip-build unless --publish-feed is explicitly needed; feed refresh can invalidate reused install proof by changing APK metadata.
Use --reuse-install-report only when the named report proves the same promoted build id and APK SHA in Android diagnostics.
If this aggregate loop lacks a needed knob, add the knob to the script instead of hand-running the same sequence again.
If input budget fails after install, inspect the aggregate report metrics before rebuilding again.

promises.claim: The Android wake-word production loop runs on the installed shell-v2 launcher, starts wake through the app bridge, applies live policy, and routes a Windows synthesized wake phrase.
promises.whenToUse: When closing or tuning the Android wake-word production loop on shell v2, after shell-v2 APK/package proof exists, or before claiming wake-word shipped.
passRequires: release feed Android build id is android-universal-20260622T193436Z or newer
Windows hot-shell preflight passes through the installed Windows app
android-native-ux-release-loop runs with --skip-build --shell-v2 --run-shell-v2-adb-proof, or responsiveness is explicitly accepted with --accept-responsiveness-incomplete
shell-v2 control relaunch uses the local Windows bridge for ADB hot ops and a key-authorized Android command origin matching the WebView/control URL origin
shell-v2 control relaunch produces a fresh current-build Android WebView v2 native-control client on the Android command origin
installed Android wake state reports build id android-universal-20260622T193436Z or newer
wake is started through start_voice_wake / WasmAgentNative.enableVoiceWake rather than direct ADB service start
apply_wake_word_policy completes through the Android app bridge
refreshed wake state reports the foreground wake service active
Windows SpeechSynthesizer stimulus runs through play_wake_phrase_probe via tools/voice/run-wake-room-loop.py
room-loop timeline includes routed voice command evidence for the synthesized wake phrase
invalidatedBy: wake model or live policy change
nextSuggestedSteps: If preflight fails with handler_timeout, fully restart/reopen the installed Windows app and rerun.
If responsiveness proof fails, fix or explicitly accept that gate before wake tuning.
If wake state build id is stale, install android-universal-20260622T193436Z or newer through the Windows bridge.
If speech produces no timeline evidence, tune live wake policy/model without rebuilding unless a missing native primitive is proven.

promises.claim: wasm-agent route resolution and map/lookup tools are registry-backed, bounded by route contracts, and do not scan source during route.resolve.
promises.whenToUse: After changing wasm-agent route contracts, direct-head route resolution, route/map/lookup tools, or provider dispatch scoping.
passRequires: route.resolve returns a declared route contract for known wasm-agent surfaces
route.resolve missing-route behavior returns route_contract_missing without invoking source search
map.summary returns a tiny route summary
lookup.files returns bounded file receipts with path, bytes, and sha256 where available
lookup.symbol searches only the resolved route contract scope
explicit workspace_root does not bypass route contract resolution
nextSuggestedSteps: If this fails, fix the route registry or resolver before allowing provider/Hermes dispatch.
Do not add route ownership strings to static_server.py; add or repair a route contract.

promises.claim: static_server.py does not route wasm-agent product ownership through CSS selectors, DOM classes, product labels, or ad hoc route regex lists.
promises.whenToUse: After editing wasm-agent provider dispatch, route contracts, UI-symbol helpers, or static_server.py routing logic.
passRequires: static_server.py does not contain WASM_AGENT_UI_ROUTE_TERMS
static_server.py does not contain WASM_AGENT_UI_SELECTOR_RE
route ownership is represented by agent_route_contracts.json or context docs, not runtime selector branches
nextSuggestedSteps: If selector routing returns, remove it and add a route contract plus resolver test instead.

promises.claim: wasm-agent persists exact provider token usage by quest, turn, and provider call, with raw usage stored separately from normalized exact fields.
promises.whenToUse: After changing direct-head provider adapters, Hermes dispatch, agent run storage, token aggregation, or timeline token events.
passRequires: direct-head provider runs require a route contract before provider/Hermes dispatch
token ledger rows are persisted per provider call
quest/run total equals the sum of exact provider-call totals
raw provider usage remains available separately from normalized exact fields
cost.status exposes compact ledger totals
nextSuggestedSteps: If exact and estimated usage are mixed, split the fields before exposing totals.
If a provider omits usage, store estimated values only under estimated fields and mark exact false.

promises.claim: avatar-chat timeline and token-ledger UI remain width-contained with long route ids, token call ids, and event details.
promises.whenToUse: After changing avatar-chat timeline rendering, token ledger rendering, agent message CSS, or compact run proof UI.
passRequires: Chromium renders synthetic long timeline rows without document horizontal overflow
agent-timeline boxes stay inside the agent message bounds
agent-token-ledger boxes stay inside the agent message bounds
long provider call ids and route ids are clipped or wrapped inside bounded grid columns
nextSuggestedSteps: If this fails, fix the layout constraints before adding more timeline or ledger fields.
Do not hide overflow by removing route, token, or proof fields from the UI.

promises.claim: A simulator user can submit a two-turn avatar-chat UI quest and receive direct-head responses with route-before-provider proof, exact quest/turn/provider-call token ledger rows, no broad Hermes fallback, and contained run timeline UI.
promises.whenToUse: After changing avatar-chat submission, direct-head route preflight, provider dispatch, token ledger persistence, or compact run timeline UI.
passRequires: simulator admin user reaches the authenticated avatar-chat UI
the UI submits exactly two provider calls to the local Responses stub
route.resolved is recorded before head.started or provider dispatch evidence for every turn
route_contract_missing is returned for objective-only routing
the exact quest ledger input, output, and total token counts equal the sum of provider stub usage across turns
each turn ledger includes exact input, output, total, quest_id, turn_id, route_id, and provider_call_id
Hermes broad fallback is absent
timeline and token-ledger UI boxes are width-contained
nextSuggestedSteps: If route proof fails, fix the declarative route contract or preflight before provider dispatch.
If token totals fail, fix exact provider usage normalization and ledger persistence before exposing UI totals.
If containment fails, fix the timeline/token-ledger layout instead of hiding proof fields.
If the simulator cannot submit deterministically, stop with the missing UI primitive instead of dispatching Hermes broadly.

promises.claim: Master:frontier route-first, tool-first, budgeted, bounded-harness production gates pass without provider or rebuild work.
promises.whenToUse: After changing Master:frontier planner, envelope, dispatch, protocol, code-memory, route contracts, provider proxy, local kernel tools, or before claiming the direct-head harness is production-ready at the static/behavioral contract layer.
passRequires: harness registry validation passes
Master:frontier planner, dispatch, protocol, envelope, prompt-audit, route-contract, intent, and code-memory tests pass
code-memory helper returns route-scoped compact results
provider proxy direct-head, local-tool, Hermes-gating, node-tool, changed-file, token-ledger, and proof tests pass
avatar-chat records stale Master:frontier reliability manifests as degraded evidence instead of blocking direct-head dispatch
wasm-agent smoke test passes
report JSON has ok true
nextSuggestedSteps: If a Master:frontier unit gate fails, fix the owning module under server/master_frontier instead of adding policy to static_server.py.
If provider-proxy fails, inspect the failing test name and repair the contract layer it names before touching provider fallback behavior.
Run with --include-runtime before claiming live node-brain availability.

promises.claim: Master:frontier can prove a named Hermes node is inspectable and answerable through wasm-agent node.capabilities and node.chat before claiming node-brain availability.
promises.whenToUse: After changing Master:frontier allowed actions, node bridge primitives, Hermes node API_SERVER_* env contracts, route contracts for Hermes nodes, or before diagnosing whether a node such as Paracelsus can answer through its own brain.
passRequires: wasm-agent bridge is reachable
target Hermes node is running
node.capabilities returns ok true for the target node
node.capabilities exposes inspect_node and run_prompt actions
node.chat returns ok true through bridge_runs
node.chat canary reply exactly matches the harness prompt
node.chat provider usage reports the expected model deepseek-v4-flash
nextSuggestedSteps: If node.capabilities fails, fix the declarative route/node bridge contract before asking the LLM to infer node state.
If node.chat fails with api_server_url_not_configured, add or repair the node API_SERVER_* env contract instead of patching Hermes core.
If the model mismatches, fix the node model/env bootstrap before claiming Paracelsus is using OpenCode Go deepseek-v4-flash.

promises.claim: avatar-chat runtime-entity history questions are resolved through declared route contracts and local kernel.inspect proof, then composed by the direct-head LLM without Hermes dispatch.
promises.whenToUse: After changing direct-head envelope routing, runtime entity route contracts, kernel.inspect, provider fallback handling, Hermes dispatch gating, or avatar-chat submission for entity/history questions.
passRequires: simulator admin user reaches the authenticated avatar-chat UI
the first submitted prompt is exactly `what is paracelsus in this code base and what has it done overtime`
the final submitted follow-up prompt is exactly `tell me more about this paracelsus meta-analsys and what it were doing`
the run completes without HTTP 502 or direct-head provider failure
Hermes dispatch is absent
the resolved runtime entity route is `hermes-node.paracelsus.runtime`
kernel.inspect runs before the direct-head LLM final answer
the final answer has exact nonzero direct-head provider token usage when the provider is available
the answer includes bounded route proof for `/local/agents/nodes/paracelsus` and `/local/datas/paracelsus`
the answer includes conversation/runtime evidence such as sessions or messages
the answer includes data investigation evidence such as summary_md_count or raw_paper_json_count
the answer does not say Paracelsus was not inspected or that runtime inspection is still needed
nextSuggestedSteps: If HTTP 502 appears, verify the running server contains the local runtime-route proof-first branch before blaming provider transport.
If Hermes dispatch appears, restore local-first runtime-entity inspection before provider or bridge work.
If route proof is missing, fix the declarative route contract or runtime entity resolver instead of adding product-specific runtime strings.
If the answer lacks conversation or data evidence, fix kernel.inspect bounded investigation before changing prompt wording.

promises.claim: avatar-chat implementation objectives that mention runtime entities route to the owned repo edit/proof lane first; runtime/entity inspection is only supporting evidence and must not preflight as the primary task.
promises.whenToUse: After changing direct-head envelope routing, runtime entity preflight, dispatch.hermes parsing, kernel projection, or implementation/action routing for avatar-chat.
passRequires: an implementation objective containing a runtime entity name resolves the owning UI route
direct-head does not emit local_runtime_route_inspection before the provider for implementation intent
kernel.inspect is not run as objective preflight for the implementation request
the provider envelope advertises implementation_uses_owned_repo_action_lane_before_entity_inspection
the direct-head final remains on the owned workspace route
dispatch targets the owned workspace route with repo.edit/test/proof capabilities
the runtime entity mention is available as supporting route evidence without becoming the primary preflight
nextSuggestedSteps: If runtime preflight appears, fix generic intent priority before adding product-specific prompt text.
If a later implementation run loses changed_files, fix scoped bridge proof collection before claiming implementation shipped.
If the provider lacks the intent-priority hint, fix the direct-head kernel projection/prompt contract.

promises.claim: The wasm-agent Agent Kernel exposes generic local-first capabilities, resolve, inspect, act, and prove primitives; direct-head can use kernel.inspect for unknown runtime/entity state without Hermes fallback.
promises.whenToUse: After changing /agent/tools kernel or route tools, direct-head local tool action handling, Hermes dispatch gating, token/proof ledgers, or the compact direct-head envelope projection.
passRequires: kernel.capabilities reports local-first primitives and bounded Hermes requirements
kernel.resolve returns a registered route contract without broad search

kernel.inspect returns bounded route-scoped observations for runtime/entity inspection, including declared capability status, run evidence, explicit unknowns, and no self-confirming model-text entity proof
kernel.act executes declared local tools and rejects external-provider dispatch
kernel.prove returns route, timeline, token-ledger, and check proof projections
direct-head local tool action handling can execute kernel.inspect before final answer
direct-head fails closed when it declares missing state/proof needs without a kernel or bounded dispatch action
Hermes is not called for locally inspectable kernel work
Hermes dispatch still requires route, capability need, budget, proof, and escalation reason
nextSuggestedSteps: If kernel primitives fail, fix the generic kernel contract before adding product-specific prompt affordances.
If unknown runtime/entity state is answered without kernel inspection, tighten the compact kernel projection or direct-head action handling.
If Hermes is called for local work, restore local-first capability gating before provider dispatch.
